action: Tap 'Deriv logo to close language menu'
observation: I see an email verification page with the title 'Check your email' and text indicating a verification link was sent to ai.test.user@example.com. There's a 'change email' link and a language selector menu that's currently open on the right side. The page is asking me to check my email for the verification link. As an elderly user, I need to proceed through the email verification process, but first I should close this language menu that's taking up screen space.
reasoning: As a 70-year-old user, I find this open language menu confusing and distracting. I need to close it first to focus on the main content. I'll tap elsewhere on the screen to close this language dropdown menu so I can better see the email verification instructions.
ux_issues: Language menu opened unexpectedly and is taking up most of the screen space
No clear way to close the language menu
Email verification page should be the focus but language menu is distracting
